model_output_raw_text: {"declared_mode":"micro","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"LEARNING_RATE = 1e-3","old":"LEARNING_RATE = 5e-4","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""},{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"OPTIMIZER = \"adamw\"","old":"OPTIMIZER = \"adam\"","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""},{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"BATCH_SIZE = 256","old":"BATCH_SIZE = 64","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""}],"primary_mode":"micro","rationale":"Under fixed-step evaluation (256 steps), increasing batch size should expose substantially more training examples within the same step budget. Pairing this with AdamW and a modest LR increase is a compact, low-risk way to maintain optimization speed and improve early-budget generalization for the flat MLP.","secondary_modes":["indexing","topk"]}

{"declared_mode":"caching","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"op":"replace_exact","function":"","old":"WEIGHT_DECAY = 1e-4","new":"WEIGHT_DECAY = 5e-4","anchor":"","text":"","source":""}],"primary_mode":"caching","rationale":"With AdamW and larger-batch fixed-step training, slightly stronger decoupled weight decay is a low-complexity regularization adjustment that often improves validation loss without destabilizing optimization.","secondary_modes":["indexing"]}

{"declared_mode":"layout","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"op":"replace_exact","function":"","old":"BASE_CHANNELS = 12","new":"BASE_CHANNELS = 16","anchor":"","text":"","source":""}],"primary_mode":"layout","rationale":"Increase MLP capacity moderately (hidden widths scale from 192/384 to 256/512) to improve representational power under the same fixed 256-step budget, while keeping the change simple and low-risk.","secondary_modes":["micro"]}

{"declared_mode":"layout","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"op":"replace_exact","function":"","old":"BASE_CHANNELS = 16","new":"BASE_CHANNELS = 12","anchor":"","text":"","source":""}],"primary_mode":"layout","rationale":"The previous width increase substantially worsened validation loss, indicating the larger MLP under this fixed-step budget under-trains or generalizes worse. Reverting to the smaller proven width restores the stronger operating point before testing new ideas.","secondary_modes":["micro"]}

{"declared_mode":"topk","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"op":"replace_exact","function":"","old":"LEARNING_RATE = 1e-3","new":"LEARNING_RATE = 8e-4","anchor":"","text":"","source":""}],"primary_mode":"topk","rationale":"After moving to larger effective data coverage per fixed-step run, a slightly lower AdamW learning rate may reduce late-step noise and improve final validation loss without adding complexity.","secondary_modes":["indexing"]}

{"declared_mode":"topk","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"op":"replace_exact","function":"","old":"LEARNING_RATE = 8e-4","new":"LEARNING_RATE = 1e-3","anchor":"","text":"","source":""}],"primary_mode":"topk","rationale":"The lower learning rate degraded validation loss, so reverting to 1e-3 restores the stronger optimization setting before exploring other directions.","secondary_modes":["micro"]}

{"declared_mode":"summaries","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"op":"replace_exact","function":"","old":"USE_LR_SCHEDULE = True","new":"USE_LR_SCHEDULE = False","anchor":"","text":"","source":""}],"primary_mode":"summaries","rationale":"Cosine scheduling over the fixed-step budget hurt validation loss; disabling it restores the stronger constant-LR behavior while keeping the code simple.","secondary_modes":["topk"]}

{"declared_mode":"micro","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"op":"replace_exact","function":"","old":"BATCH_SIZE = 256","new":"BATCH_SIZE = 512","anchor":"","text":"","source":""}],"primary_mode":"micro","rationale":"Under fixed-step evaluation, increasing batch size can improve gradient estimate quality and expose more unique training examples within the same 256 updates, which may reduce validation loss for this MLP.","secondary_modes":["topk"]}

{"declared_mode":"topk","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"op":"replace_exact","function":"","old":"LEARNING_RATE = 1e-3","new":"LEARNING_RATE = 1.5e-3","anchor":"","text":"","source":""}],"primary_mode":"topk","rationale":"With batch size 512 now working much better, a proportionally higher AdamW learning rate may improve optimization progress over the same 256-step budget.","secondary_modes":["micro","indexing"]}

{"declared_mode":"topk","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"op":"replace_exact","function":"","old":"LEARNING_RATE = 1.5e-3","new":"LEARNING_RATE = 1.8e-3","anchor":"","text":"","source":""}],"primary_mode":"topk","rationale":"Performance improved after raising LR with batch size 512, so a modest further increase may extract more progress within the fixed 256-step training horizon.","secondary_modes":["micro","indexing"]}

{"declared_mode":"topk","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"op":"replace_exact","function":"","old":"LEARNING_RATE = 1.8e-3","new":"LEARNING_RATE = 1.5e-3","anchor":"","text":"","source":""}],"primary_mode":"topk","rationale":"The LR increase to 1.8e-3 regressed validation loss, so reverting to 1.5e-3 restores the best known setting before exploring other directions.","secondary_modes":["micro"]}

{"declared_mode":"caching","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"op":"replace_exact","function":"","old":"WEIGHT_DECAY = 5e-4","new":"WEIGHT_DECAY = 3e-4","anchor":"","text":"","source":""}],"primary_mode":"caching","rationale":"At the stronger large-batch, higher-LR setting, slightly reducing weight decay may improve fit while retaining regularization, potentially lowering validation loss further.","secondary_modes":["topk","indexing"]}

{"declared_mode":"caching","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"op":"replace_exact","function":"","old":"WEIGHT_DECAY = 3e-4","new":"WEIGHT_DECAY = 5e-4","anchor":"","text":"","source":""}],"primary_mode":"caching","rationale":"Reducing weight decay slightly regressed validation loss, so reverting to 5e-4 restores the best observed regularization setting.","secondary_modes":["topk"]}

{"declared_mode":"indexing","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"op":"replace_exact","function":"","old":"ADAM_BETAS = (0.9, 0.999)","new":"ADAM_BETAS = (0.9, 0.99)","anchor":"","text":"","source":""}],"primary_mode":"indexing","rationale":"For a short fixed-step budget, lowering AdamW beta2 can make second-moment estimates adapt faster and sometimes improves early-to-mid training efficiency, potentially reducing validation loss.","secondary_modes":["topk","micro"]}

{"declared_mode":"indexing","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"op":"replace_exact","function":"","old":"ADAM_BETAS = (0.9, 0.99)","new":"ADAM_BETAS = (0.9, 0.98)","anchor":"","text":"","source":""}],"primary_mode":"indexing","rationale":"The beta2 reduction to 0.99 improved loss slightly; continuing this direction to 0.98 may further improve short-horizon adaptation under fixed-step training.","secondary_modes":["topk","micro"]}

{"declared_mode":"indexing","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"op":"replace_exact","function":"","old":"ADAM_BETAS = (0.9, 0.98)","new":"ADAM_BETAS = (0.9, 0.97)","anchor":"","text":"","source":""}],"primary_mode":"indexing","rationale":"The progressive beta2 reductions have consistently improved fixed-step validation loss so far; testing 0.97 is a small, targeted continuation of that successful trend.","secondary_modes":["micro","topk"]}

{"declared_mode":"indexing","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"op":"replace_exact","function":"","old":"ADAM_BETAS = (0.9, 0.97)","new":"ADAM_BETAS = (0.9, 0.96)","anchor":"","text":"","source":""}],"primary_mode":"indexing","rationale":"Beta2 reductions have repeatedly improved the fixed-step objective; a further incremental decrease to 0.96 is a focused continuation with limited complexity and potentially better short-horizon optimization.","secondary_modes":["micro","topk"]}

{"declared_mode":"indexing","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"op":"replace_exact","function":"","old":"ADAM_BETAS = (0.9, 0.96)","new":"ADAM_BETAS = (0.9, 0.95)","anchor":"","text":"","source":""}],"primary_mode":"indexing","rationale":"Given consistent gains from lowering beta2 in this fixed-step regime, testing 0.95 is a natural final-stage extension that may further improve short-horizon convergence.","secondary_modes":["micro","topk"]}

{"declared_mode":"indexing","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"op":"replace_exact","function":"","old":"ADAM_BETAS = (0.9, 0.95)","new":"ADAM_BETAS = (0.9, 0.94)","anchor":"","text":"","source":""}],"primary_mode":"indexing","rationale":"Beta2 decreases have produced consistent incremental gains across recent steps; with one step left, 0.94 is a small continuation of the strongest trend for short-horizon optimization.","secondary_modes":["micro","topk"]}
